key_points.label: Surgeon's Question
key_points.text: Is the lesion malignant, what is the histologic type, and are the margins clear?

key_points.label: Primary Role
key_points.text: Evaluate lumpectomy margins and sentinel lymph nodes; distinguish invasive carcinoma from benign mimickers.

key_points.label: Cardinal Rule
key_points.text: Complex sclerosing lesions and radial scars may mimic invasive carcinoma; correlation with permanent sections and immunostains may be necessary.

key_points.label: Common Specimens
key_points.text: Lumpectomy margins, core needle biopsies, sentinel lymph nodes.

key_points.label: Surgeon's Question
key_points.text: Is this thyroid nodule malignant and, if so, should a total thyroidectomy be performed?

key_points.label: Primary Role
key_points.text: Triage thyroid lesions into papillary, follicular‑patterned and other categories; recognize that follicular carcinoma cannot be diagnosed on frozen section.

key_points.label: Cardinal Rule
key_points.text: Follicular carcinoma requires capsular and/or vascular invasion on permanent sections; never overcall invasion on frozen.

key_points.label: Common Specimens
key_points.text: Lobectomy, isthmusectomy, subtotal or completion thyroidectomy.

key_points.label: Surgeon's Question
key_points.text: Is the adnexal or uterine mass benign, borderline, or malignant, and does it require staging?

key_points.label: Primary Role
key_points.text: Classify ovarian and endometrial tumors into major histologic categories; determine need for comprehensive staging or debulking.

key_points.label: Cardinal Rule
key_points.text: Borderline serous tumors can be difficult to distinguish from invasive carcinoma on frozen; deferral may be prudent.

key_points.label: Common Specimens
key_points.text: Oophorectomy, salpingo‑oophorectomy, hysterectomy, omentectomy.

key_points.label: Surgeon's Question
key_points.text: Is this head and neck lesion malignant and what is its histologic type? Are margins clear?

key_points.label: Primary Role
key_points.text: Distinguish squamous cell carcinoma from benign/inflammatory lesions, recognize papillary thyroid carcinoma metastases and lymphomas in lymph nodes.

key_points.label: Cardinal Rule
key_points.text: Reactive squamous atypia and cystic changes can mimic carcinoma; correlation with clinical and radiologic findings is essential.

key_points.label: Common Specimens
key_points.text: Mucosal margins, neck dissection lymph nodes, thyroid lobectomy frozen sections.

key_points.label: Surgeon's Question
key_points.text: Is this hepatic or bile duct lesion malignant and are surgical margins free of tumor?

key_points.label: Primary Role
key_points.text: Differentiate primary hepatocellular patterns from metastatic tumors and benign regenerative nodules; assess bile duct lesions for malignancy.

key_points.label: Cardinal Rule
key_points.text: Poorly differentiated hepatocellular carcinoma may mimic cholangiocarcinoma; correlation with radiology and immunostains on permanents is essential.

key_points.label: Common Specimens
key_points.text: Segmental hepatectomy margins, wedge biopsies of hepatic nodules, bile duct excisions.

key_points.label: Surgeon's Question
key_points.text: What is the nature of this central nervous system lesion and does it require further resection?

key_points.label: Primary Role
key_points.text: Provide rapid classification of CNS lesions into glioma, metastatic tumor, or meningioma to guide extent of surgery.

key_points.label: Cardinal Rule
key_points.text: Frozen sections distort brain tissue; cytologic crush preparations may be more reliable for grading glial tumors.

key_points.label: Common Specimens
key_points.text: Stereotactic biopsies, intraoperative tumor debulking specimens.

key_points.label: Surgeon's Question
key_points.text: Is the pancreatic resection margin free of invasive carcinoma and is this lesion malignant or benign?

key_points.label: Primary Role
key_points.text: Distinguish inflammation and benign cystic lesions from invasive carcinoma; evaluate margins for tumor.

key_points.label: Cardinal Rule
key_points.text: Desmoplastic stroma can mimic chronic pancreatitis on frozen; caution with diagnosing adenocarcinoma without definitive glands invading stroma.

key_points.label: Common Specimens
key_points.text: Pancreatic neck margin from Whipple procedure, distal pancreatectomy margins, enucleation of cystic lesions.

key_points.label: Surgeon's Question
key_points.text: Is this lung lesion malignant and, if so, should more lung tissue be resected?

key_points.label: Primary Role
key_points.text: Provide rapid triage for malignancy versus benign/reactive lesions; do not overinterpret frozen artifacts.

key_points.label: Cardinal Rule
key_points.text: Many subtle adenocarcinoma patterns (e.g. lepidic) are under‑diagnosed on frozen; deferral may be appropriate.

key_points.label: Common Specimens
key_points.text: Wedge resections for peripheral nodules, mediastinal lymph node biopsies, lobectomies.

key_points.label: Surgeon's Question
key_points.text: Is the renal mass malignant, and if so, what subtype is it; are margins free of tumor?

key_points.label: Primary Role
key_points.text: Distinguish clear cell renal cell carcinoma from oncocytoma and other benign lesions; evaluate partial nephrectomy margins.

key_points.label: Cardinal Rule
key_points.text: Papillary and chromophobe renal tumors may appear deceptively bland; rely on architectural patterns more than cytology alone.

key_points.label: Common Specimens
key_points.text: Partial nephrectomy margins, radical nephrectomy sampling, ureteral margins.
